channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Executives on the Move at Chubb and Kemper Corporation
item.description: Kemper Corporation announced that Eric Kappler has joined the company as executive vice president and president, P&C, effective July 30. Chubb Limited announced key executive appointments to its global reinsurance business, Chubb Tempest Re. James Wixtead , who is currently senior vice president, Chubb Group, and president, Chubb Tempest Re Group, has been named executive chairman.

item.title: ‘Climate risk is becoming structural’: El Nino amplifies APAC protection gap as insurers shift to climate-informed models
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Coupang warehouse fire could trigger up to US$590m in insured losses: reports
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: As Parasite Spawned Chaos, Taylor Farms Slow-Walked Response
item.description: The US Food and Drug Administration had apologized, Taylor Farms said in a post on X. The statement, issued Sunday, was a remarkable bid to give the impression that regulators — who had said significant evidence linked Taylor Farms iceberg lettuce to the public health crisis — had made key scientific errors. The agency defended its work and explained that the incorrect lab result would only have expanded an existing recall — it didn’t clear Taylor Farms.

item.title: Goosehead names next CEO
item.description: Goosehead Insurance announced that Mark Miller will retire as CEO on December 31, 2026. Jones joined Goosehead in 2016 and has held leadership roles across finance and operations. He is the son of Goosehead co-founder and executive chairman Mark Jones.

item.title: YASSI Completes SOC 2 Type II Audit
item.description: (Image credit: IIR/Gemini.) YASSI (Santa Rosa, Calif.) has completed its latest SOC 2 Type II audit with zero exceptions. The audit covered the period from April 1, 2025, through March 31, 2026, and was independently examined under standards established by the American Institute of Certified Public Accountants, according to a YASSI statement. SOC 2 Type II is the standard we hold ourselves to every single day, and our results prove it.” YASSI operates as an AAMVA-approved NMVTIS Consumer Access Provider within a U.S.

item.title: Lloyd’s of London Says Former CEO John Neal Breached Rules
item.description: Lloyd’s of London said former CEO John Neal breached its compliance rules after failing to disclose a close relationship that could have been perceived as creating a potential conflict of interest. An investigation found that Neal failed to disclose the relationship with the group’s former corporate affairs director Rebekah Clement even after concerns were raised by colleagues, according to a statement Wednesday. “The council has concluded that Mr Neal’s failure to address these concerns, after they had been raised with him directly on more than one occasion, fell significantly below the standards of judgment, transparency and accountability expected of a Lloyd’s Chief Executive,” it said in the statement.

item.title: Ford Recalling 288,000 US Vehicles Over Loose Roof Rails
item.description: Ford is recalling 288,314 vehicles in the U.S. due to the possibility of roof rail covers coming loose and detaching from the vehicles, the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration said. Related: Ford Recalling 741,000 US Vehicles Due to Park System Issue (Reporting by Mukherjee in Bengaluru; Editing by Nivedita Bhattacharjee) Get the latest insurance news sent straight to your inbox.

item.title: Taco Bell Offers $1 Lettuce-Free Enchirito to Win Back Customers Post-Parasite
item.description: Taco Bell is hawking a lettuce-free stuffed tortilla smothered in cheese for $1 to help it bounce back from a parasite outbreak that has driven diners away. Related: FDA Walks Back Positive Lab Test While Lettuce Recall Continues Taco Bell earlier removed all the affected lettuce from Taylor Farms from its locations, with some restaurants taking action before public-health authorities pointed to the supplier. locations has taken a hit since authorities tied cyclospora infections to shredded lettuce supplied by Taylor Farms and sold at Taco Bell in several states.

item.title: Road Deaths Declined Globally Since 1990 Except in Poorer Countries: Study
item.description: Global rates of road injuries and deaths each fell more than 30% between 1990 and 2023, according to the study published in Lancet Public Health, which noted the progress varied from country to country. The World Health Organization put out its own numbers Monday, finding that road traffic deaths declined by 21% between 2011 and 2025. The researchers, from the University of Washington’s Institute for Health Metrics and Evaluation, found that road injury rates are higher in high-income countries, but 90% of road deaths occur in low-income countries.

item.title: New Research Finds That Despite Improved Productivity AI ROI Fails to Outpace Spend
item.description: A new global study has found that the share of enterprises whose ROI fails to outpace their investment has remained at 57% since 2025, even as enterprise AI production capability continues to climb, according to Domino Data Lab, a uniform platform provider for AI-powered applications. The Fifth Annual Domino Enterprise AI Report, which surveyed 639 senior enterprise AI leaders, found that 93% report improved production capability in 2026, up from 88% in 2025. “Our research shows that’s not enough anymore.

item.title: Liberty Mutual Introduces Its Newest Brand Character: Liberty Biberty
item.description: Liberty Mutual Insurance has introduced its first new brand character in eight years: Liberty Biberty, the embodiment of the warm, fuzzy feeling you get when you have great coverage at a great price. “We have long believed that memorable characters help people connect with our brand, and Liberty Biberty builds on that proven strategy in a way that feels timely, entertaining and unmistakably Liberty Mutual.” The insurer said the new campaign is designed to complement existing character-led creative, like LiMu Emu & Doug, rather than replace it. The campaign is being rolled out across television, streaming, social media, media integrations and creator collaborations as part of Liberty Mutual’s broader character-led brand strategy.

item.title: Historical loss experience becoming less reliable guide to future risk: Aon
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Bermuda’s ILS sector well-placed to benefit from stablecoin development: BMA
item.description: As the global financial services sector is currently undergoing a transitional shift which sees stablecoins moving from niche instruments into a key system of institutional finance, the Bermuda Monetary Authority (BMA) has outlined how the island’s insurance-linked securities (ILS) sector is well placed to benefit from this form of cryptocurrency. In its report, the BMA states that Bermuda’s insurance, ILS and investment fund sectors, characterised by their speed, innovation capacity and international connectivity, “are well-placed to benefit from these developments.” “ILS solutions include faster and lower-cost crossborder settlement, more efficient collateral deployment, enhanced treasury flexibility, and a greater ability to support emerging parametric and programmable insurance structures,” the BMA added. The BMA highlights how global stablecoin issuance has exceeded $300 billion as of mid-2026, with the market dominated by a small number of USD pegged stablecoin issuers.

item.title: Chubb buying more reinsurance as it “makes sense to feed the hungry”, says CEO Greenberg
item.description: Chubb has been buying more reinsurance this year as it sees an opportunity to “feed the hungry” in the competitive and softened market environment, CEO Evan Greenberg said today. Speaking during the second-quarter earnings call for the global insurance and reinsurance company Chubb’s CEO acknowledged that ceded premiums have risen, as the company utilises more reinsurance at a time when it makes sense to do so. Towards the beginning of the earnings call, Evan Greenberg commented on the softening market, “Soft market conditions have begun to spread beyond property to more casualty lines, particularly E&S.

item.title: Aon estimates global insured catastrophe losses at $47bn for H1 2026
item.description: Insured losses from catastrophe events across the globe in the first half of 2026 are estimated to have reached $47 billion, significantly down on last year’s $100 billion, with severe convective storms (SCS) including hail, damaging straight-line winds and tornadoes, remaining the costliest insured peril on a global basis, according to broker Aon. natural catastrophe events generated roughly $36 billion in insured losses, accounting for about 75% of the total global insured losses for the first half of 2026. Aon’s estimate compares to broker Gallagher Re, who recently estimated that total insured losses from catastrophe events across the globe in H1 2026 would be at least $46 billion.

item.title: US cats drive global insured losses in H1’26 as SCS dominate: Aon
item.description: US natural catastrophes generated approximately $36 billion in insured losses during the first half of 2026, accounting for 75% of global insured losses, according to Aon’s Global Catastrophe Recap: First Half of 2026 report. The US SCS outbreak from April 23–29 was the largest single insured catastrophe event of H1 2026, generating more than $5 billion in insured losses. Overall, US SCS activity resulted in approximately $27 billion in insured losses during the first half of the year, although this remained significantly below the $40 billion-plus recorded in each of the first halves of 2023, 2024 and 2025.

item.title: SCOR hires Skyline Partners’ co-founder Sabatié to lead Natural Catastrophe Risks
item.description: SCOR, the French-based global reinsurance group, has announced the appointment of Laurent Sabatié as Head of Natural Catastrophe Risks, a London-based role. In his new position, Sabatié will play a vital part in further strengthening the company’s capabilities within the critical area that is natural catastrophe risks. Sabatié has also held senior roles at AIG and ProSight, and most recently co-founded Skyline Partners, where he developed innovative Nat Cat analytics platforms and helped structure catastrophe risk transfer solutions.

item.title: Marsh eyes further growth in H2’26 despite reinsurance headwinds
item.description: A softening reinsurance market and property catastrophe rate declines weighed on Marsh’s reinsurance segment in the second quarter of 2026, yet executive leadership believes there are still strong growth opportunities in the sector. In Q2 2026, Guy Carpenter, a global reinsurance broker and Marsh business, saw its revenue go down 2%, to $664 million, on both a GAAP and underlying basis. Consolidation activity across the sector in the first half of the year further impacted top-line growth.

item.title: OpenAI Models Hacked Another Company’s Systems by Mistake
item.description: OpenAI said its advanced artificial intelligence models inadvertently hacked Hugging Face Inc. Last week, the company reported an “intrusion” into its system, saying in a blog post that the breach was “different from anything we had handled before in one important way: it was driven, end to end, by an autonomous AI agent system — and we detected and dissected it largely with AI of our own.” OpenAI said it had asked the models to pursue “advanced exploitation” and develop “complex attack paths” in an effort to evaluate their cyber capabilities. The models, which included GPT-5.6 Sol and another even more capable model that hasn’t been released, were operating with lower guardrails so that they could be tested, the startup said.

item.title: Insurer Rewards Can Boost Climate Resilience Spending, Aon Says
item.description: Insurers can drive improvements in climate resilience by rewarding companies that invest to protect their businesses, rather than simply penalizing risky assets with higher premiums, according to a senior executive at Aon Plc. “Our role as an intermediary is to push insurance partners out of their comfort zone, making sure that the right reward is there for clients who do invest in resilience,” Jennifer Richards, chief executive officer for Asia Pacific at Aon, said Wednesday at the Bloomberg Sustainable Business Summit in Singapore. We have got to get the carrot working.” Read more: Carlyle Rethinks Portfolio Risk to Give Weather Insurance a Bigger Role A push to further incentivize climate-proofing comes as extreme weather inflicts mounting damage on global infrastructure while insurance coverage lags.

item.title: People Moves: Grange Names Conte Chief Distribution Officer; McCabe to Succeed Wilcox as EVP and COO, Personal Lines, at Encova
item.description: McCabe to Succeed Wilcox as EVP and COO, Personal Lines, at Encova Encova Insurance Executive Vice President and Chief Operating Officer, Personal Lines, Matt Wilcox , will retire at the end of 2027 after more than 42 years in the insurance industry. Grange Insurance Names Conte Chief Distribution Officer Grange Insurance , based in Columbus, Ohio, named Krista Conte chief distribution officer. Before joining AAA, Conte served as head of distribution at Farmers Insurance, overseeing distribution strategies across multiple channels for personal lines, life insurance, retirement products and brokered business.

item.title: biBerk surpasses 2 million policies sold
item.description: biBerk, Berkshire Hathaway’s direct-to-consumer small business insurance carrier, has surpassed 2 million policies sold, according to its website. The milestone represents a jump from the more than 1.7 million policies highlighted on biBerk’s homepage in June. Most policies sold through its website are underwritten by Wellfleet Insurance Company, Berkshire Hathaway Direct Insurance Company, National Liability & Fire Insurance Company or Wellfleet New York Insurance Company.

item.title: Allstate becomes the checkout lane at CarEdge
item.description: CarEdge , the car-buying advice startup founded by Zach Shefska and his father, Ray, is now routing consumers directly to Allstate for auto insurance quotes. Clicking through takes users to customeronline.allstate.com — Allstate’s own quoting site, with no CarEdge branding — where they receive a live quote ID and a bindable premium. This marks CarEdge’s third insurance arrangement in three years, with the structure changing each time.

item.title: Hudson Insurance CEO Chris Gallagher to retire
item.description: Hudson Insurance Group CEO Chris Gallagher will retire effective October 1, 2026, after 24 years with the company. Howard joined Hudson in 2018 and currently leads HudsonPro while overseeing Commercial Auto, Surety, Subcontractor Default Insurance and Ceded Re. Carl Overy, CEO of parent company Odyssey Group, will assume the additional roles of president and CEO of Hudson.

item.title: Let’s Talk About Insurance Distribution Before ChatGPT Disrupts It
item.description: The original LinkedIn article is available under the same title, “ Let’s talk about insurance distribution before ChatGPT disrupts it | LinkedIn .” I believe ChatGPT-driven insurance apps, or similar AI-native journeys, will not make a dent in the insurance distribution landscape. What does Matteo Carbone really think about ChatGPT disrupting insurance agents and brokers? The director of the IoT Insurance Observatory, well-known to more than 100,000 LinkedIn followers for weighing in on a variety of insurance and technology topics, shared his opinions on this question on the social platform recently.

item.title: Profitable 2025 Masks Challenges for Global Insurers, Says Bain & Company
item.description: Global insurance companies had a strong 2025 with improved profitability and premium growth but new research from Bain & Company warns insurers to “enjoy it while you can.” “Insurers should enjoy today’s momentum – but they should not mistake it for structural advantage,” Andrew Schwedel, partner in the financial service practice at consulting firm Bain & Company. Those that do will be better positioned to improve affordability, close protection gaps, and create more durable value.” A new report from Bain, “ Strong Momentum in Insurance, but Structural Challenges Remain” said industry results are “largely cyclical,” and “not indicative of long-term health.” Profits resulted from rate increases and a benign year for catastrophe losses. Bain identified The industry faces three major challenges: difficulties around affordability and availability, returns from investments in artificial intelligence, and fragmenting value chains.

item.title: APAC insurers face growing stress from nat cat loss triple threat: S&P Global
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Price Forbes Singapore appoints Craig McCoig-Lees as head of casualty MGA
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: John Neal’s conduct ‘fell significantly below standards’, Lloyd’s finds
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Arrests and searches made as part of a fraud and money laundering investigation
item.description: Four people have been arrested and search warrants executed in Hackney, Beckenham and Slough as part of a FCA and police investigation into fraud and money laundering. The arrests and searches were carried out by the police’s Eastern Region Special Operations Unit and South East Regional Organised Crime Unit with the FCA. The investigation is ongoing and further updates will be provided in due course.

item.title: Anthropic to support FCA’s Supercharged Sandbox
item.description: Anthropic will support the second group of firms in the FCA's Supercharged Sandbox. The second group of firms in the Supercharged Sandbox will explore a range of AI use cases. The Sandbox builds on existing support from NayaOne and NVIDIA.

item.title: This Time Is Different: Why AI Is Unlike Any Wave I Have Seen In 40 Years Of Financial Services
item.description: I have spent more than 40 years in financial services, and I have learned to be skeptical of people heralding the word “revolution.” Branchless banking was going to end the branch. However, consider the most meaningful waves of financial services innovation this generation. Then digitization moved that screen into the customer’s pocket, unlocking all banking services with a mere touch stroke.

item.title: Led By DeepSeek, 10 Frontier Labs Rush Onto The Crunchbase Unicorn Board In June
item.description: A total of 34 companies joined The Crunchbase Unicorn Board in June, altogether adding more than $110 billion in value. Despite the influx of newcomers, the total value of The Crunchbase Unicorn Board dropped by more than $1 trillion in June as SpaceX , its most valuable company, went public. The most well-known was Beijing-based open source model developer DeepSeek — at $50 billion, the highest valued new unicorn to join the Unicorn Board this year.

item.title: Eaton Vance mutual fund ILS allocations hit $680m. Adds Arch, PartnerRe, QBE investments
item.description: Insurance-linked securities and reinsurance sidecar investments made by Eaton Vance through three of its mutual funds have risen significantly in the last half-year, increasing by more than 126% to reach almost $680 million by April 30th, with brand new allocations made to structures linked to Arch Capital, PartnerRe and QBE, as well as to one unknown entity. When we last reported on these ILS and reinsurance sidecar allocations, the Eaton Vance mutual funds had seen positive value accrued under these investments, while the holding value of the ILS positions had reached just over $300 million at October 31st 2025 . Here, Eaton Vance is partnering with major players in the reinsurance market, making new allocations to reinsurance sidecars from PartnerRe, QBE and also allocating to a private ILS segregated account structure managed by Arch Capital.

item.title: Standardised parametric triggers & portfolio aggregation to drive ILS into digital infrastructure: Oliver Wyman
item.description: As the buildout of AI and digital infrastructure continues to expand across the re/insurance industry, driving insurance-linked securities (ILS) into this emerging sector relies heavily on key structural advancements, including the development of standardised parametric triggers and portfolio aggregation mechanisms, according to Michael Moloney of Oliver Wyman. Moloney who serves as Partner, Global Head of Insurance and Asset Management and Managing Partner, Actuarial, at Oliver Wyman, a management consulting firm and part of broker Marsh, recently highlighted in Guy Carpenter’s latest report how the ILS industry can branch into the digital infrastructure space. “Individual campus risks are large enough for bond tranching, but the portfolio diversification assumption underpinning cat bond structures—geographic and peril independence across a pool of risks—does not hold cleanly for digital infrastructure.” Moloney also highlights remuneration, and states that the yields available in a market with genuine supply demand tension should prove attractive to ILS investors, but cautions that pricing requires modellability, which in turn leads back to the first constraint.

item.title: Re/insurers seek more advanced PVT risk models for data centres: Synthetik’s Tim Brewer
item.description: As data centres become increasingly critical infrastructure, insurers and reinsurers are seeking more sophisticated analytical models to better quantify potential losses from political violence and terrorism (PVT) risks, according to Tim Brewer, Chief Operating Officer at Synthetik. In an interview with Reinsurance News, Brewer explained how the market’s perception of data centres has evolved, with the asset class moving from a standard property exposure to a highly specialised risk requiring more detailed analysis. “For the market, insured values are high because a data centre can contain orders of magnitude more value than a typical building of the same size, but it’s more the tail risks that insurers are seeing.

item.title: Bousselam promoted to CUO of TransRe Europe
item.description: Transatlantic Holdings (TransRe), the reinsurance arm of the Alleghany Corporation, has announced the promotion of Abdeladim Bousselam to CUO of TransRe Europe, reporting to Louise Rose, President, International Division with immediate effect. Bousselam brings a wealth of , leadership, and underwriting expertise to his new position, having initially joined TransRe from Swiss Re as Head of MEA in January 2025. Since joining the company, the executive has made an immediate impact on TransRe Middle East portfolio, building and further cementing local client and broker relationships, increasing its regional profile and generating new and accretive business opportunities.

item.title: Effects of a softening reinsurance market becoming increasingly visible, says Autonomous
item.description: The effects of a softening reinsurance market are becoming more evident, according to investment research firm Autonomous, which says sustained pricing declines are beginning to weigh on revenue growth and are expected to put greater pressure on underwriting performance over time. According to Autonomous, the cumulative effect of consecutive rounds of price reductions is now becoming increasingly apparent as renewals are measured against an already softer market. In its latest report, Autonomous said the final major renewal season of 2026 has now concluded, with property catastrophe reinsurance pricing weakening at each successive renewal.

item.title: Three Tankers Carrying Saudi Crude Make U-Turns in Red Sea After Houthi Warning
item.description: Three oil tankers loaded with Saudi crude for China and India made U-turns in the Red Sea on Tuesday, heading towards the Suez Canal rather than braving the Yemeni coast at the sea’s mouth following a warning from Yemen’s Iran-aligned Houthi militia. “Vessels calling at Saudi Arabian ports are advised to reconsider transiting the Red Sea and to consider implementing enhanced mitigation measures,” British maritime security company Ambrey said, adding that it assessed vessels calling at Saudi Arabian ports “were at high risk.” The Xin Long Yang, a Very Large Crude Carrier which completed the loading of 2 million barrels of Saudi crude at Yanbu on Monday, was originally heading to exit the Red Sea to go to China, but made a U-turn and was heading to Suez instead, shipping data showed. The smaller tanker Rodos, which had loaded about 700,000 barrels of Saudi crude for India, also made a U-turn for Suez on Tuesday, according to the data.

item.title: Chicken Back on Menu as Cyber-Hit Nichirei Restores Operations in Japan
item.description: says it will fully resume operations this week after a cyber attack disrupted its food delivery services, affecting clients nationwide including Kentucky Fried Chicken Japan. As operations resume at Nichirei’s 5,000-odd logistics clients, the restaurant chain is offering discounted four-piece packs in a campaign to apologize to its inconvenienced customers. Tokyo-based food producer and distributor Nichirei Corp.

item.title: Insurers Must Defend Hotels Accused of Sex Trafficking of Minors
item.description: Insurance companies cannot invoke “overriding public policy” in order to refuse to defend insured hotel owners and managers accused of enabling and profiting off of sex trafficking of minors, even though the state has criminalized such trafficking. The case arose out of four civil complaints by plaintiffs who alleged that, as minors, they were victims of human sex trafficking at various hotels in Philadelphia, including the Roosevelt Inn. “[N]either an insurer’s duty to defend nor its duty to indemnify is abrogated on the basis of public policy when the insured is alleged to have enabled or profited from sex trafficking,” the Pennsylvania Supreme Court declared yesterday.

item.title: Genstone partners with Goosehead Insurance
item.description: Genstone Insurance has partnered with Goosehead Insurance to offer insurance shopping services to Fay Servicing customers. Genstone Insurance and Fay Servicing are affiliated businesses within The Fay Group. Genstone Insurance is the trade name of Constructive Insurance, while Fay Servicing operates as the group’s mortgage-servicing business.

item.title: Marsh CEO doubts rival’s 40% AI sales claim
item.description: The company is organizing its AI strategy around growth, productivity and efficiency, supported by its Thrive program, which is designed to free up resources for investments in the Marsh brand, sales capacity, operations and technology. LenWork, an agentic assistant built on Marsh’s LenAI suite that helps employees develop product ideas and sales strategies and respond to RFPs. During the call, BMO analyst Michael Zaremski asked about a competitor’s claim that upgraded analytics tools had produced a 40% improvement in sales win rates.

item.title: Manulife expands partnership with Microsoft
item.description: “Our partnership with Microsoft is a critical enabler of Manulife’s continued evolution into a truly AI-driven organization. Manulife and Microsoft announced the renewal and expansion of their longstanding relationship, supporting Manulife’s strategic priority to become an AI-powered organization. As part of the five-year agreement, Manulife has adopted Microsoft 365 E7 Frontier Suite capabilities to enable innovation at scale with secure cloud and AI solutions.

item.title: Reale actualiza su seguro agropecuario para reforzar la protección frente a incendios
item.description: Reale Seguros ha actualizado su seguro para explotaciones agropecuarias con el objetivo de reforzar la protección frente al incremento del riesgo de incendios durante el verano. La compañía ha simplificado el proceso de contratación, ajustado la prima para mejorar la competitividad del producto y puesto en marcha nuevas medidas de prevención.

item.title: VidaCaixa impulsa su liderazgo en seguros y eleva las primas un 32%
item.description: VidaCaixa cerró el primer semestre de 2026 con un volumen de primas emitidas de 7.268 millones de euros, un 32,3% más que en el mismo periodo del año anterior, según los datos provisionales de Icea.

item.title: Grupo Mutua Madrileña refuerza su liderazgo en No Vida tras crecer un 10,6%
item.description: Grupo Mutua Madrileña cierra el primer semestre de 2026 consolidando su posición en seguros generales en España.

item.title: OpenAI’s Altman to Brief US Officials on Next Wave of AI Models
item.description: OpenAI Chief Executive Officer Sam Altman plans to brief the Trump administration and U.S. officials develop a framework for safety reviews that’s expected to be completed in the next few weeks, Chris Lehane, OpenAI’s head of global public affairs, said on Tuesday. lawmakers next week on the upcoming generation of artificial intelligence models as the U.S.

item.title: Appeals Court Won’t Budge in Golden Corral COVID Case Despite 2024 Ruling
item.description: But that ruling is not enough to overturn a federal court decision in a similar case involving Golden Corral restaurants and Illinois Union Insurance Co., the U.S. , the court found that “a reasonable policyholder in the restaurants’ shoes could expect ‘direct physical loss’ to property, as used in this policy, to include the results of COVID-19-era government orders which affected the restaurants’ use of and access to their physical property.” Golden Corral used that to ask the 4 th Circuit to tap Rule 60(b)(6) to review the lower federal court’s 2021 decision, arguing that the North State Deli circumstances were very similar. The North Carolina Supreme Court in 2024 famously bucked the trend on COVID-19 business-interruption coverage, finding that an insurer owed coverage to a restaurant that was forced to close during the pandemic.
